national.notes: For crimes, accidents, and public safety emergencies. Operators generally speak Mandarin; ask for English assistance if needed.
Medical emergencies. Alternatively call 999 in some cities. Be prepared to give your exact location in Mandarin or have someone nearby assist.
Fire and rescue emergencies. Also assists with non-fire rescues (e.g., trapped persons, animal rescue).
National tourism complaint and information hotline. Available in English in major tourist cities. For travel-related disputes, lost passports guidance, and tourist services.

cityHotlines.notes: Shanghai Foreign Affairs Service Hotline. English-speaking operators available for foreigners needing assistance with daily life, emergencies, and city services.
Beijing Municipal Government Service Hotline. Multilingual support including English for city services, complaints, and general inquiries.
Guangzhou Government Service Hotline. Offers assistance in English and other languages for public services, complaints, and emergency guidance.
Shenzhen Government Service Hotline. Foreign-language assistance available. Covers city services, emergency referrals, and expatriate support.
Chengdu Government Service Hotline. English-language support available for tourists and residents covering city services and emergency information.
